---
title: "Forecasts"
subtitle: "Model predictions and generalization across countries"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
metrics = FileAttachment("_data/metrics.json").json()
gapData = FileAttachment("_data/gap_data.json").json()
pipelineConfigs = FileAttachment("_data/pipeline_configs.json").json()
Plot = import("https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@observablehq/plot@0.6/+esm")

countryTargets = ({
  "norway": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "canada": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "retail_sales", "unemployment"],
  "sweden": ["cpi", "industrial_production", "unemployment"]
})
```

## Per-variable test performance

Select a country to see how the agent-tuned and zero-shot configurations compare to baselines on each target variable across the test era.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fCountry = Inputs.radio(
  ["norway", "canada", "sweden"],
  {label: "Country", value: "norway",
   format: v => ({"norway": "Norway", "canada": "Canada", "sweden": "Sweden"})[v]}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fVariable = Inputs.select(
  countryTargets[fCountry],
  {label: "Variable", value: "cpi",
   format: v => v.replace(/_/g, " ").replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase())}
)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-variable-test
//| fig-cap: "Test-era MASE by horizon for the selected variable. MASE < 1 beats the random walk."

{
  const showMethods = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];
  const methodColors = {
    "Random Walk": "#95a5a6",
    "ARIMA": "#2ecc71",
    "BVAR": "#3498db",
    "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)": "#9b59b6",
    "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)": "#e74c3c"
  };

  const data = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === fCountry && d.era === "test"
    && d.target === fVariable && showMethods.includes(d.method)
    && d.mase != null
  ).map(d => ({
    method: d.display_name,
    horizon: d.horizon,
    mase: d.mase
  }));

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 380,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 45,
    x: {label: "Forecast horizon (months)", ticks: [1, 3, 6, 12]},
    y: {label: "MASE", grid: true},
    color: {
      domain: Object.keys(methodColors),
      range: Object.values(methodColors)
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4"}),
      Plot.line(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", stroke: "method",
        strokeWidth: 2.5, curve: "monotone-x"
      }),
      Plot.dot(data, {
        x: "horizon", y: "mase", fill: "method",
        r: 5, tip: true
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

## Per-variable test table

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
{
  const testData = metrics.filter(d =>
    d.country === fCountry && d.era === "test" && d.target === fVariable
  );

  const methodOrder = ["random_walk", "arima", "bvar", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];
  const methodSet = new Set(testData.map(d => d.method));
  const methods = methodOrder.filter(m => methodSet.has(m));

  const rows = methods.map(method => {
    const mData = testData.filter(d => d.method === method);
    const row = {Method: mData[0]?.display_name || method};
    for (const h of [1, 3, 6, 12]) {
      const d = mData.find(d => d.horizon === h);
      row[`h=${h}`] = d?.mase?.toFixed(3) || "---";
    }
    return row;
  });

  return Inputs.table(rows, {
    columns: ["Method", "h=1", "h=3", "h=6", "h=12"],
    width: {Method: 220}
  });
}
```


## Cross-country comparison

How does each method's average test-era MASE compare across the three countries?

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-cross-country
//| fig-cap: "Average test MASE across all horizons for key methods, by country."

{
  const showMethods = ["arima", "bvar", "zero_shot", "agent_tuned"];
  const methodNames = {
    arima: "ARIMA", bvar: "BVAR",
    zero_shot: "Chronos-2 (zero-shot)", agent_tuned: "Chronos-2 (agent-tuned)"
  };

  const data = [];
  for (const country of ["norway", "canada", "sweden"]) {
    const targets = countryTargets[country];
    for (const method of showMethods) {
      const vals = metrics.filter(d =>
        d.country === country && d.era === "test"
        && d.method === method && targets.includes(d.target)
        && d.mase != null
      ).map(d => d.mase);
      if (vals.length > 0) {
        data.push({
          country: country.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + country.slice(1),
          method: methodNames[method],
          mase: vals.reduce((a,b) => a+b, 0) / vals.length
        });
      }
    }
  }

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 350,
    marginLeft: 60,
    marginBottom: 50,
    x: {label: null, padding: 0.3},
    y: {label: "Average Test MASE", grid: true},
    fx: {label: null},
    color: {
      domain: Object.values(methodNames),
      range: ["#2ecc71", "#3498db", "#9b59b6", "#e74c3c"],
      legend: true
    },
    marks: [
      Plot.ruleY([1.0], {stroke: "#999", strokeDasharray: "6,4"}),
      Plot.barY(data, Plot.groupX({y: "identity"}, {
        x: "method", y: "mase", fill: "method", fx: "country",
        tip: true
      }))
    ]
  });
}
```

**Key patterns:**

- **Norway:** Agent-tuned config hurts --- worse than zero-shot on the test era. Fine-tuning overfits to the validation-era covariate relationships.
- **Canada:** Agent-tuned config helps slightly --- Chronos-2 (both variants) strongly beats ARIMA and BVAR.
- **Sweden:** Agent-tuned config gives the biggest improvement, without fine-tuning --- covariate selection and context truncation generalize well.
